feed.title: SAP Community - Careers
feed.subtitle: Careers blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP Certification Transformation: How You Can Get and Stay Certified
entry.content:
It’s no secret: Technology is changing faster than most businesses can manage. Well-trained talent is increasingly scarce, and digital skills gaps keep getting wider. To keep up with the ongoing changes in talent and technology, both learners and organizations need to be agile and adaptive to keep pace with the competition.   

At SAP, our mission is to create opportunity through learning and development for all. And with the need for fast and comprehensive upskilling, we are transforming our SAP Certification program, making continuous learning a priority. That way, you can always prove you’re up-to-date with the latest innovations. 

Here’s what this means for you: 

Get certified with trusted certifications, tailored to your role. 

Transforming our certification approach means making things easier for you. Demonstrate intermediate or advanced skills with out of over 80 globally recognized role- and product-based certifications across key SAP solution innovation areas. Focusing on the core skills and competencies, our role-based certifications, like this one for SAP S/4HANA Cloud implementation consultants, make your skillset more valuable as it gains sustainability and more relevance for longer periods of time.   
Good news: the enhanced SAP Learning Hub includes four certification exam attempts per subscriber per year and all learning resources to prepare for the certification exams. 
 

Stay certified and keep your skills up to date with the latest innovations. 

As a second step in our certification transformation, we are focusing on opportunities for you to keep your skills sharp. As of this April, certified learners need to continuously keep their skills up-to-date and certifications valid with evolving products by completing mandatory assessments before their certification expires. With an SAP Learning Hub subscription, you can prepare for and take the stay certified assessments at any time during the validity period of your certification. 

The standard validity of certifications is 12 months, with an initial validity end date of March 31, 2025. Every time you complete an assessment to stay certified, the validity period extends by 12 months. Easy enough, right? 

For sure! So, this is how you can keep your certifications valid: 

  1. Log into the ‘My Learning’ page with your SAP Universal ID (Note: your SAP Universal ID must be linked with a secondary email account from another email domain provider) 
  2. With a click on “Certifications” section you will find your passed certification(s) with the respective validity date.  
  3. Access the enablement materials that are linked to prepare for the stay certified assessment. You need an SAP Learning Hub subscription to access stay certified assessment. 
  4. Take the stay certified assessment linked to your certification. Once successfully passed, the new validity date of your certification will be updated. 

If you are already certified in an SAP solution, you will receive a personalized email informing you about the required next steps to ensure that you don’t miss your certification expiry date. So don’t worry, we’ve got you covered! 

We truly believe that the redesigned SAP Certification program will make it easier and faster for you to get and stay certified, to identify next career steps and goals, and ensure you have the latest skills and valid credentials on the newest SAP innovations tailored to your role and responsibility. With a subscription to the enhanced SAP Learning Hub, you can have access to all the learning resources you need to get and stay certified in one place. So why don’t you set yourself up for success? 

For more information, please visit the SAP Certification transformation page.  

Please share any feedback or questions you have! 

#SAP Certification #SAP Learning Hub

entry.title: Get Inspired by Baiba's Success Story with SAP
entry.content: Discover the inspiring journey of Baiba Blumberga, a determined young woman who navigated the challenges of an economic crisis and found her true calling in the tech industry – thanks to SAP. From studying European economics to becoming a skilled ABAP developer, Baiba's story is a testament to the power of resilience and adaptability. Read more about her experiences, the importance of continuous learning, and the impact of SAP Certification on her career. And get ready to be motivated and start your own success story with SAP today!

entry.title: Ashvini's Journey: Reimagining Success through SAP in a New Country
entry.content:
When Ashvini Kulkarni arrived in the US in December 2022, the skies over Boston were mostly grey. They might as well have been telling the story on the ground. The country was facing the widely expected recession following the pandemic. The news tickers on the airport TV were running headlines about tech layoffs, climbing mortgage rates and an uncharacteristically warm winter. “Quite a welcome!”, Ashvini thought.  

Feeling empowered to stand shoulder to shoulder with professionals who have years of industry experience: Ashvini.Hailing from the former princely city-state of Bhopal in India, it was not the first time Ashvini had flown far from her nest. Watching her father work on SAP kindled her interest in the Enterprise Ressource Planning (ERP) software. “My decision to pursue a career in SAP was heavily influenced by my father who happened to be an SAP user at one of the world-renowned multinational companies,” the 38-year-old explains. “His insights were invaluable, and they helped me realize the vast potential of SAP in the business world.” She soon found herself in Pune, working on SAP Business Planning and Consolidation (SAP BPC) for a Europe-based manufacturing client, was invited by her customers to go to Sweden, and later to Australia. Until things took a turn.  

Building a new professional identity from scratch

Ashvini got married and relocated to Boston in the United States, where her husband already lived. “In this new environment, I faced the challenge of building my professional identity from scratch,” she recalls. And despite her open work permit, the hiring freeze many companies put into place didn't help. With 12 years of experience in the field of SAP Business Planning and Consolidation consulting, Ashvini had a lot to show: a track record of various clients from diverse industries across the globe, many successfully completed implementation projects, numerous awards and appreciation from employers and clients, and a cosmopolitan mindset that only frequent travel could get you. But it wasn’t enough for Ashvini.  

To understand why, you need to know how Ashvini ticks. “I am deeply committed to continuous growth and self-improvement,” she says, “education and skill development remain a central focus, as I aspire to acquire advanced knowledge in my field and expand my skill set to excel professionally.” While traveling and exploration hold a special place in her heart, and immersing herself in new cultures, creating lasting memories, is important to her, personal growth and self-improvement are ongoing goals. “I aim to nurture my emotional intelligence, develop a deeper sense of empathy, and cultivate a growth mindset to enhance both my personal and professional life,” Ashvini summarizes.

SAP's digital skills initiative helped her get certified

And this is why she decided to SAP’s digital skills initiative. “Relocating to the U.S. prompted me to invest in continuous learning and skill development. Pursuing SAP software skills aligns with my goal of adapting to a new job market and building a strong professional identity in a new country.” So, she opted for a certification in SAP Analytics Cloud Planning – successfully passing the exam. 

Keen to try her hand at her newly acquired skill, the hunt for SAP Analytics Cloud roles and opportunities began. “A company named Forage was offering a simulation project with compelling exercises and technical challenges,” she says. Sensing an opportunity to keep her skills warm, Ashvini spent the next few weeks working in the simulation environment, exploring and testing SAP Analytics Cloud from every conceivable angle. 

Feeling empowered

Ashvini immediately sensed the extent of her decision to take the exam. “Obtaining the SAP Certification has been a remarkable milestone in my career. It has not only enhanced my technical knowledge but also boosted my self-confidence, particularly as I find myself navigating a new country and job market,” she says. “In a place where I'm starting fresh, this certification serves as a testament to my commitment to excel in the field of SAP.”  

Feeling empowered to stand shoulder to shoulder with professionals who have years of industry experience, she says she now feels confident to take on any challenge. “I am excited to explore new opportunities in the U.S. with this valuable SAP Analytics Cloud Planning certification as a key asset in my arsenal.” Knowing that employers value the verified skills she has, Ashvini is restarting her career. “Having an SAP Certification on my resume has made me a more attractive candidate to employers and increased my employability.” And it all started with her father laying the foundation for her success so many years ago.  

Start your own success story

Did you feel inspired by Ashvini’s story? Then kick-start your own success story with SAP today. Benefit from tailored and self-paced learning opportunities that meet you when and where you learn best on SAP Learning site. Get certified to demonstrate your expertise and boost your career. We are looking forward to many more remarkable success stories! 

Are you looking to hire people like Ashvini? Reach out to the digital skills initiative for information on how to contact her and other people like her who are entering the SAP ecosystem with a fresh cloud-first mindset. We are happy to connect any partner or customer with our candidates to provide a mutual growth opportunity for new talent and the organizations supporting our ecosystem.

entry.title: Como Iniciar uma Carreira SAP com dicas essenciais
entry.content:
Você já ouviu falar sobre SAP e está interessado em iniciar sua carreira nessa área emocionante e em constante crescimento? Você veio ao lugar certo! Neste blog, vamos explorar algumas dicas essenciais para profissionais que desejam dar os primeiros passos na carreira SAP.

1. Conheça o Mercado de SAP

O primeiro passo para iniciar sua carreira em SAP é entender o mercado e suas demandas. O SAP é um dos principais sistemas de software empresarial do mundo, utilizado por milhares de empresas em todo o globo. Isso significa que há uma grande demanda por profissionais qualificados em SAP, desde consultores até desenvolvedores e analistas.

2. Educação e Certificações

Investir em educação e certificações é fundamental para se destacar no mercado SAP. Existem vários cursos e certificações disponíveis, que abrangem diferentes áreas do SAP, como FI (Financeiro), MM (Gestão de Materiais), SD (Vendas e Distribuição), entre outros. Certifique-se de escolher um curso que seja relevante para suas metas de carreira e que seja reconhecido pela indústria.

3. Experiência Prática

Além da educação formal, a experiência prática é crucial para se tornar um profissional SAP bem-sucedido. Procure oportunidades de estágio, projetos acadêmicos ou mesmo voluntariado em eventos e palestras, é o principal, atuar profissionalmente em empresas que usem o SAP. Isso lhe dará uma exposição valiosa ao sistema e ajudará a construir seu portfólio.

4. Mantenha-se Atualizado

O mundo do SAP está em constante evolução, com novas atualizações e tecnologias sendo lançadas regularmente. É importante que você se mantenha atualizado com as últimas tendências e desenvolvimentos do SAP, participando de cursos de reciclagem, conferências e grupos de discussão online.

5. Networking

Por fim, não subestime o poder do networking na indústria SAP. Participe de eventos, conferências e grupos de discussão online para conhecer outros profissionais da área, trocar experiências e aprender com os melhores. O networking pode abrir portas para oportunidades de emprego e parcerias comerciais no futuro.

Em resumo, iniciar uma carreira em SAP requer dedicação, educação e experiência prática. Ao seguir essas dicas essenciais, você estará no caminho certo para se tornar um profissional SAP de sucesso. Boa sorte em sua jornada!

Saibam mais sobre essas e outras dicas de como se tornar um consultor SAP com o vídeo Abaixo:

https://youtube.com/live/vHpCZ6Vc-i8

 Se você tiver alguma dúvida ou gostaria de compartilhar suas próprias experiências sobre iniciar na carreira SAP, sinta-se à vontade para deixar um comentário abaixo.

entry.title: Building trust: The foundation of effective leadership
entry.content:
Image: SAP Image Library

A leader becomes a key factor during changing times, driving organizations, and creating a sense of safety across their teams. A trusted leader creates a collaborative environment where people generate value sustaining the corporate strategy and making the work environment creative, supportive, and transparent.

A leader who acknowledges their weakness and development needs become closer to their team and creates a sense of flatness, where the appointed person supports the individual contribution and drives their team to accomplish the unit´s goals via collaborative decision-making. When the people in their team feel that a leader is closer to them with needs, aspirations, and challenges, they are open to sharing their requirements and then creating a strong and trustworthy relationship. Hence, getting closer to the team´s needs supports individual growth and improves confidence in individual members, a trusted leader is aware of their team´s feelings, needs, and dreams. Trust is the basis of leadership, and keep consistency about values behaviors, and performance.

"Getting closer to the team´s needs supports individual growth and improves confidence in individual members."

A trusted leader is consistent with their actions, supporting their reliability and predictability, hence the team becomes comfortable when their leader´s decision-making, establishes a sense of safety and consistency. The persons are prone to perform better where their work environments are healthy and feeling less internal pressures support a high performing activities, at the same time decreases the probability of stress-related sickness, whose consequences could be mental or physical illness, in the long run a healthy environment also produces company benefits in terms of productivity and people´s attainment, reflected on less rotation and better work-life balance positioning in the industry.

Leadership growth shall be observed as a process, where learning opportunities are available from different sources, it is fundamental to align the company expectations, the values and behaviors, and expected performance. Leadership awareness becomes fundamental to skill up trustability, especially when challenging situations that measure and snapshot a manager´s behavior. Since each action over the team creates individual career fingerprints, it is important to be consistent with all team members, avoiding preferences or differential reactions because a manager could create a distorted perception about the abilities of each person in their team.

Besides self-awareness, self-regulation becomes a natural consequence, where the leader´s adaptability to different situations produces reactions that require wearing the other´s shoes to avoid over-reactions. When a manager becomes exposed to the same situation and their reaction does not improve, their lead capabilities decrease and negatively affect the people´s interactions. Therefore, evolving conscious about the circumstances and improving the reactions, creates a sense of trust in the teams and stakeholders, for course acknowledging errors and being transparent about the corrective actions makes a leader closer to the team.

Building trust requires a bi-directional action, where the leader and their team members must be transparent, coherent, and trustable about their activities and actions. This combination supports the corporate strategy and ultimately contributes to the team´s performance, which is always perceived as a puzzle where everyone contributes to the common results, without higher weight and the leader is the person in charge of presenting those results as the unity for their team and not as a personal result while the leader is being accountable, each person is responsible for their contribution.

A trustworthy leader is a person who is always present, especially when there are issues or failures, passing the failure token to the team shall not happen. In difficult times the leader is the visible face of the team, hence confronting the situation and assuming responsibility is the natural action, without avoiding the confrontation, a corrective plan must be proposed, and the corresponding action to improve the situation. Recognizing achievements are public actions while addressing errors are private discussions with their team members.

Building trust requires an additional effort, while a manager holds several responsibilities, among those is the commitment to improve their team´s cohesion and sense of belonging, including diversity and inclusion. The natural process requires practice, observation, and continuous learning, where the leader´s awareness about their needs drives the learning path to set up specific skills development.

A leader who allows open communication and becomes approachable creates trust among their team, peers, customers, and stakeholders. While acting with integrity, the actions and performance reflect the company's values and behaviors. An empathic leader allows open communication with a strong sense of authenticity, avoiding despaired behaviors with upper management, pairs, and teams. A trustable leader openly communicates the expectations creating a sense of task ownership and the expected future accomplishments across their team members. 

Nurturing an organic feedback process increases performance management while addressing requirements to achieve small victories, a leader promotes a trusted and consistent environment, where the team openly shares their toughs and challenges. The result of an organic feedback process becomes the foundation of realistic improvement plans with detailed and measurable actions in the short term.

A leader´s commitment makes people better and helps them develop further abilities required to accomplish their tasks via appropriate delegation and autonomy development through an accountable decision-making process. And, to help them to prepare for the next career movement. The coaching process requires empathy and a common understanding of the expectations, capabilities, and development willingness. A trusted leader takes care of the people and team, understanding their limitations and how each person in the group complements the requirements to make it successful.

Finally, to create a competitive company a trustworthy leader creates an appropriate environment to grow their team supported by transparency and accountability, where a shared vision nurtures continuous improvement to support personal growth while supporting organizational goals.

entry.title: Breaking Down Barriers: Hear from Chelcie Scott How She Broke into Tech with the Help of SAP
entry.content:
“Being a young parent is very hard”, Chelcie recounts. She was 20 when her daughter Eva was born, moving to Atlanta just one year later. Chelcie wanted to prioritize her little girl, but also needed to make a living as a single mother. She wanted her daughter to be proud of her. “I always wanted to be the example I wish I had”, she says. So, she moved to Atlanta, hoping for a better life.  

Starting to think bigger 

Chelcie soon started her own business as a performance coach. She still remembers the many highs and lows the challenge of being a single mother and entrepreneur all in one. “Having an income that’s not always reliable – it’s difficult. I’ve always enjoyed entrepreneurship, but it always comes with a certain cost of things.” She was in survival mode, as she says, and thinking bigger wasn’t in the cards for her as she tried to make ends meet. Until she was offered the chance to pivot into the tech industry.  

With the help from Rejoice Jones from Vower, an organisation which aims to connect communities with non-traditional employers, and SAP’s digital skills initiative, Chelcie joined an SAP S/4HANA bootcamp. “I want to change the trajectory for my family, to show my daughter that women, especially that black women have the capacity and skills to work in these spaces and sectors”, she says, aware of the unshakably low figures. After all, with only 27% of all computing-related jobs held by women, and the number for people from ethnic minorities dropping even lower, accessing digital skills and securing a fulfilling, well-paid job in the tech industry often seems out of reach for most of the world’s population.

Digital skills initiative provides free learning and certification 

“But Rejoice and SAP encouraged me to imagine and think bigger”, Chelcie explains. “I grew up in a low-income household. I was the first generation to go to college.” Knowing that you are just not exposed to what’s out there, you don’t always have the contacts and don’t always see people like you in those positions, she explains.

Designed to bridge the gap between providing easily accessible training and certifications for everyone considered underrepresented in the technology industry and supporting organizations with their workforce transformation and search for fresh talent, SAP’s digital skills initiative contributes to a more diverse and equitable job market, workforce, and society as a whole. “There are also so many barriers into these industries which is why the SAP free bootcamp and certification was so amazing for allowing me to take the next step”, Chelcie confirms.  

To learn more about Chelcie’s story, watch the video above – happy ending guaranteed!

Start your own success story today 

And if you want to follow in her footsteps, kick-start your own success story with SAP today. Benefit from tailored and self-paced learning opportunities that meet you when and where you learn best on SAP Learning site. Get certified to demonstrate your expertise and boost your career. We are looking forward to many more great success stories! 

Are you looking to hire people like Chelcie? Reach out to the digital skills initiative for information on how to get in contact with her and other people like her who are entering the SAP ecosystem with a fresh cloud-first mindset. We are happy to connect any partner or customer with our candidates to provide a mutual growth opportunity for new talent and the organizations supporting our ecosystem.

entry.title: From Holland to Harrods: Beau’s Inspiring Journey in Tech and Transportation
entry.content:
Being English and Dutch, Beau used to visit his grandparents in the Netherlands often when he was young. With his grandparents living in a farming village, it was no surprise that he developed a passion for transport, logistics and agricultural vehicles at an early stage in his life. “I dreamt of owning my own transport company with my twin brother Max, called Twin Transport, in which we would transport all types of vehicles”, the now 22-year-old explains. He has no doubts: “I think my dream and passion for supply chain and logistics really stemmed from there”.  

To ensure he continuously learns and develops his supply chain and logistics knowledge, Beau has developed a great network with senior leaders in the logistics industry at a U.K. and global level and frequently visits transport distribution centers, exhibitions, railway ports and OEMs. He also dedicated his formal education to his favorite topic, studying International Business Management with a specialization in Supply Chain and Logistics, at Amsterdam University of Applied Sciences. Being part of the SAP University Alliances program, the institution provides access to teaching and learning environments for SAP solutions, as well as digital learning resources for students and professors to upskill in the latest solutions and prepare for SAP Certification exams.  

University Lecturers Talked About High Demand for SAP Skills 

“Starting my degree in 2020 during COVID, it quickly became apparent that taking the SAP Honors Module alongside my university degree was a great opportunity to strengthen my future credentials. Back then, he says, he knew that tech and specifically SAP was in increased demand. With his lecturers making it clear, the huge demand for SAP consultants, module knowledge and certifications. “Realizing that there was a massive industry out there for me, I knew I needed to pursue SAP further in the early stages of my career”.

Following the biggest values in his life – Passion and Consistency, as well as Being Curious and Not Judgmental – Beau not only stayed on track with his studies but was also determined to obtain an SAP Certification. “To learn about SAP solutions was a real eye opener to me, identifying how many business processes can be integrated within one business solution”, he recounts. “I gained a real insight into how an SAP system can control many day-to-day business processes varying from HR, production, procurement to transportation. I knew this was the technological side of the supply chain that I needed to delve into.” 

Proud to Attach SAP Certification to CV 

As his confidence grew in the exam’s theoretical aspect, Beau was also able to apply it to practical business projects during his university modules. “So, I really had the support of both ends and realized it was a no-brainer during my degree and early stages in my career to take the SAP exam”, he explains. He graduated from university with a first-class BA honors degree and an SAP certification to go with it. “It was even better to hear from my university, that I was one of the very few to do both the practical and theoretical exam in one go, both smashing them with very high scores”, he states.  Adding, “I firmly believe taking an SAP Certification was a great decision during university, as it helped kick-start my career and made my CV very impressive at young age”.

Working for a Famous Department Store and Campaign 

Last September 2023, Beau started as a Supply Chain Graduate for the well-known luxury department store Harrods, in the United Kingdom. His first placement included being assistant Retail Manager for the Harrods Hamper department, where he successfully supported the dispatch of thousands of customer orders to the UK and internationally. In managing and overseeing a team of sales associates and operatives, ensuring the day-to-day operational retail running, and utilizing SAP solutions to make, amend, and cancel orders, Beau helped to achieve “the best record-breaking year for hampers”, he proudly states. 

Of course, Beau hasn’t forgotten about his ‘Twin Transport’ dream. “My twin brother works in the railway industry” he explains. “So, combining rail with transport and logistics gives us a massive future together: It also means we can share our stories, learn from each other and have someone along the journey to share the great success. It’s great having him with me”. 

Next up for Beau is pursuing another SAP Certification in SAP S/4HANA. “As Harrods continues to invest in SAP and S/4HANA, it will allow him to share his SAP knowledge, contribute to business projects and progress his career development at Harrods”. 

What’s in it for you? 

Did you feel inspired by Beau’s story? Then kick-start your own success story with SAP today. Benefit from tailored and self-paced learning opportunities that meet you when and where you learn best on the SAP Learning site. Get certified to demonstrate your expertise and boost your career. We are looking forward to many more great success stories! 

Find out more about how we enable the next generation to learn, research, and innovate with business applications for the Intelligent Enterprise here.

entry.title: Ignite Your Passion for Technology: Become an SAP Certified Enterprise Architecture Consultant
entry.content:
There’s no doubt: New technologies like Artificial Intelligence rapidly transform the way we work – and will continue to do so. We see this in our private life, where playing with image editing picks up speed, where we generate songs for our friends and family, or simply ask an AI to write a letter for us. But we also see digital transformation and change in the businesses we work in. Imagine being able to design, structure and fine-tune the backbone of an organization with new tools. It’s enterprise architects who are responsible for this task – and you can become one of them.  

Using SAP LeanIX to excel at your job 

To be an outstanding enterprise architect, you need to gain profound knowledge and sharp skills in core competencies and tasks. And of course, you want to be able to demonstrate them, too, to make sure (future) employers can have confidence in you. If that’s what you’re looking for, then look no further. SAP has got you covered.   

Check out our brand-new role-based certification SAP Certified Associate - Enterprise Architecture Consultant - SAP LeanIX. This certification validates that you possess enterprise architecture fundamental skills and core knowledge in using SAP LeanIX required for the consultant profile. By getting certified, you can demonstrate that you have an overall understanding and in‐depth skills to participate as a member of project team with a focus on enterprise architecture using SAP LeanIX. 

SAP LeanIX embodies an innovative take on Enterprise Architecture, allowing accurate visioning, planning, and implementation of IT landscapes. This tool suitably complements the enterprise architecture and can prove fundamental in successful transformation projects in organizations. 

Start with this free learning journey 

Getting started is easy. Just look at the new free, self-paced learning journey "Learning to Use the LeanIX Tool for Successful Enterprise Architecture" on the SAP Learning site. In just 5 hours, you’ll get a deep understanding of LeanIX features, its set up and will be introduced to not one, but two practical use cases. No matter if you are a data scientist, project manager, consultant, administrator, or architect – this course is for you.  

Open new professional opportunities for you with new learning and a sought-after certification. Build the skills you need on the SAP Learning site and maximize your learning experience by joining the SAP Learning Groups, to ask questions from SAP experts and connect with fellow learners. When you’re ready, take your certification exam and start shaping your industry's future!

entry.title: A suggestion on how to approach networking and a career search in the SAP ecosystem
entry.content:
Hi Folks,

For people who are new to SAP, getting to know more experienced members of the ecosystem can sometimes be a challenge. Finding a career opportunity, even more difficult. I wanted to share a recommendation for newcomers on how to approach the SAP career & development space in a useful way.

Note: If your sole purpose is landing any lucrative job you can find, no matter what it is about, consider skipping this blog. This may not be for you.

 

I’d like to share one basic rule of networking in the SAP community: steer clear from overt job requests. Do not share an air of desperation, but rather of curiosity (if you have it).

 Let's delve into some specifics:

 

Make the first move

Introduce yourself on the SAP Community and actively reach out to experts in areas you are interested in. This is what a curious person would do. A critical mistake of newcomers is their lack of engagement on the SAP Community and other social media platforms. Get your name seen and do not fear asking “dumb” questions.

  • Join the career corner here - speak with people who are recruiting
  • Join the learning group here - ask lots of questions
  • Use the SAP Community search, search for keywords that interest you, then tag the people who seem to know something about that topic with your questions.
  • Add an avatar image of yourself. The majority of people are hiding behind an anonymous silhouette icon - you will differentiate yourself from them and be more memorable this way.
  • Do the same thing across social media platforms - be seen.

 

Seek the personal experiences of others

Genuine curiosity is a great conversation starter. Make it a habit to ask about the personal experiences of those who have walked down the road you are embarking on.

  • How did they get started in SAP?
  • What challenges did they face along the way?
  • What were they thinking on day 1 of their first project?
  • Did they experience a fair amount of mentorship as a beginner?

 

Focus on In-depth Questions

 

When you manage to strike up a conversation with an SAP specialist, try to evoke insightful responses by asking about their particular area of expertise. For example, you might ask:

  • What is the first thing they try to convey to a customer before they begin their SAP implementation?
  • Could you give me some real-world examples of how an implementation of SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (for example) made a material difference in the way your customer operates?
  • Who in the company benefitted the most? What were their processes like before, during and after the project?
  • Who had the hardest time? Were their difficulties technical or cultural?

 

Solution-specific personal stories provide insight on what to specialize in

 

Invite people to share practical examples or case studies from their work. You might ask:

  • How do they think their geographical location influenced what they specialized in?
  • Do they recommend their area of specialization for a beginner?
  • Their success stories and even their failures can shed some light on the path that lies ahead of you. Ask about them.
  • What unique challenges does their area of expertise present in terms of the team dynamic?

Put yourselves in the shoes of the people doing these jobs- really try to understand their experience from their eyes.

 

Express Genuine Interest

 

By focusing on listening and learning, you convey that you are genuinely interested in SAP, rather than just seeking employment opportunities. Share a little about your journey too - why are you drawn to SAP? What excites you about it? Personal stories make social interactions memorable.

 

 Final thoughts

Remember that networking is a two-way street. Stay open and receptive to ideas, thoughts, and constructive criticism. Your goal is not to land a job immediately, but to create meaningful connections and gradually become a part of the SAP community.  

 Above all, have patience. Genuine relationships take time to build. But when opportunities do come, they stem from mutual trust and respect, making them truly worthwhile.

 Thanks for reading and good luck on your journey!

 -David

entry.title: From Grief to Growth: How Hamza’s Perseverance Helped Him Become an SAP Professional
entry.content:
When his father died, Hamza stepped up and out of his comfort zone to be the best big brother and father figure he could be. “It was then when I realized that I should never stop learning new things and having new experiences in my personal and professional life. I knew I had to keep moving forward to be successful and to be a role model,” the 28-year-old from Tunisia explains. “Central to my ethos is the belief that knowledge is the cornerstone of success. Thus, I am perpetually in pursuit of learning, seeking to broaden my horizons and enhance my skills.” And that is when he stumbled upon SAP. 

Immediately hooked by SAP learning resources

“It all began with a quest to find the appropriate software,” Hamza remembers. “As I explored various options, I stumbled upon SAP and was captivated not only by the wealthy resources available online but also by the miscellaneous features that the learning resources provided.” Hamza was immediately hooked. He took a deep dive into the world of SAP, went from beginner level courses to implementing SAP solutions and specializing in consultancy. Up to this day, he has completed more than 20 programs, earned records of achievements and certifications proving his valuable knowledge – and making his resume more than respectable. However, Hamza still was not content. 

“Through this journey, I honed my skills in client engagement, solution design, and hands-on implementation,” Hamza says.“My thirst for knowledge persisted. I yearned to comprehend the entire consulting process comprehensively, from inception to completion,” he explains in hindsight. And then he stumbled over SAP again. To be precise, it was the “Becoming an SAP Professional” course on Coursera, one of the world’s largest online learning platforms, that caught his attention. Being part of an online, seven-course SAP Technology Consultant Professional Certificate, Hamza could learn even more about SAP, the role of a technology consultant, and the value SAP professionals bring to customers. “Through this journey, I honed my skills in client engagement, solution design, and hands-on implementation,” Hamza says. “The pinnacle of this education endeavor was a practical project where I applied my expertise to create solutions tailored to real business needs.” And it paid off quickly. 

Now he shares his knowledge and skills with others

Just recently, Hamza embraced a new role as a Content Creator, through "Let's Make Tunisia Better, “ a program aimed at promoting knowledge sharing and professional development in Tunisia, where he immerses himself in the complexities of functional consulting, honing his skills and expertise with every Topics. “It's not just a role, it’s a journey where each challenge becomes an opportunity for growth and innovation,” he says, convinced that without his latest certification, this would not have been that easy. 

“For those aspiring to craft a strategic SAP career development plan, I wholeheartedly recommend prioritizing this certificate. It not only refines your skills but also prepares you for the upcoming chapters in your professional journey,” Hamza states from his own experience: “The pivotal moment in my life is to secure the position of an SAP consultant. Given my responsibilities towards my family, this achievement will not just be a career milestone but a dream to fulfill. I owe this accomplishment to my perseverance and, significantly, to my certificates.” 

Start your own success story today

Did you feel inspired by Hamza’s story? Then kick-start your own success story with SAP today. Benefit from tailored and self-paced learning opportunities that meet you when and where you learn best on SAP Learning site. Get certified to demonstrate your expertise and boost your career. We are looking forward to many more great success stories!

entry.title: New Flexible Enterprise Architecture Training Starting in September
entry.content:
As organizations continue to shift more of their workload to the cloud, the role of the enterprise architect is becoming increasingly vital. Bridging the gap between business and technology, enterprise architects ensure companies can reach their desired business outcomes through the effective use of technology. Recent numbers prove the demand for this role: It was ranked as the number one best career on Glassdoor recently – highlighting high job satisfaction and a competitive salary. 

Recognizing the advantages that this role can offer both individuals and organizations, SAP has a long tradition of delivering best-in-class enterprise architecture training. However, many of you asked for a more flexible format to learn these skills – and we’re happy to share that a new offering is now available for enterprise architects: 

Introducing a new cohort-based, blended training 

Get ready to level up your game with our new cohort-based blended enterprise architect learning! Combining the flexibility of self-paced learning on the SAP Learning site with interactive and fun case study training in a series of live sessions, you can now get the best of both worlds to prepare for your SAP Enterprise Architect Certification. 

Sounds good? Here’s what you need to know: 

To get started, head over to the SAP Learning site and register for one series of live sessions. Each series consists of six individual sessions that take about 2,5 hours each. The first cohorts will start on September 16 with two time slots available to serve your personal time zone. You don’t have time in September? Don’t worry – further cohorts will start in early November and registration is already open. 

First cohort starts in September 

Once you’re registered, all you need to do is wait for your kick-off event. The weekly sessions will be moderated and guided by an experienced speaker and delivered via Zoom. The series of live sessions will be based on a case study and built on each other – so make sure you don’t miss a session! 

Working through the units within the learning journey is essential as preparation for the sessions with the speaker. In the sessions with the trainer, the participants work on practical examples in a case study. The speaker does not teach any theory; this is taught in the learning journey “Exploring the SAP Enterprise Architecture Framework Foundation”. 

On the days where no live session takes place, you can deepen your knowledge with the new learning journey “Exploring the SAP Enterprise Architecture Framework Foundation”. Available mid-September, the course will teach you how the SAP Enterprise Architecture Framework is structured and how you can tailor it to your needs. After finishing the five units, you’ll be able to create an architecture vision of capabilities and business values, define a target business architecture to achieve business goals, and more. In just under 16 hours, this course will deepen your good understanding of business strategy, organization, and process concepts. 

Schedule your certification exam individually 

Your SAP Certification exam can then be scheduled individually after the last live session has taken place and your learning journey has been completed – so only five weeks after you started training! And of course, sharing your new badge as SAP Certified Professional - SAP Enterprise Architect is highly recommended to demonstrate your latest and comprehensive understanding of SAP solutions to help businesses use technology to meet their visions. 

Let’s talk about prerequisites 

To make sure you can make the most out of this cohort-based, blended training, the learning journey and live sessions are designed for architects with an intermediate proficiency level who possess TOGAF (The Open Group Architecture Framework) foundation knowledge, are TOGAF-certified and have a good understanding of IT strategy, applications, data, and technology concepts. Please note that this offering is included in a subscription to the enhanced SAP Learning Hub only.  

Sign up for the series of live sessions today and get ready to bring out your best with a new, flexible enterprise architecture training!

entry.title: Registration For SAP TechEd Virtual In 2024 Is Now Live!
entry.content:
It’s time to bring out your best again and join us for this year’s SAP TechEd Virtual! The high-energy, interactive and virtual experience is accessible to everyone and will take place on October 8-9, 2024. Spoiler: Registrants will benefit from an amazing goodie this year – so read on! 

Here’s what you can expect from SAP TechEd Virtual 

SAP TechEd is all about innovation and collaboration. By joining the virtual experience, you can gain the skills and expertise needed to advance your career and help your organization thrive using SAP technologies. Get ready to explore new tech, learn from industry leaders, and discover best practices from SAP experts and partners. Plus: Hear firsthand customer accounts and join a vibrant, growing community of peers. 

Deep dive into 6 SAP TechEd tracks 

At SAP TechEd Virtual, you can choose from six content tracks covering SAP technologies, products, and solutions, each offering a variety of sessions and formats: 

  • Digital Transformation with Cloud ERP 
  • SAP Business Technology Platform – General 
  • Application Development and Automation 
  • Data and Analytics 
  • Integration 
  • Artificial Intelligence 

As always, the packed agenda includes keynotes, expert sessions with our Developer Advocates, lectures, and more. The full session catalog is now available.

SAP TechEd goes on tour 

To continue your learning journey beyond SAP TechEd Virtual, you can also participate in one of our in-person SAP TechEd on Tour stops and an additional event hosted by SAPinsider EMEA. 

Get excited for Devtoberfest 

Can’t get enough of SAP TechEd? Then don’t worry, we've got you covered. To extend the spirit of SAP TechEd even further and enable everyone in executing sought-after development skills all year round, Devtoberfest will become Devtoberfest by SAP TechEd and take place September 23 - October 4 and October 14 - 25. Block your calendars today to learn, code, collaborate, and earn the chance to win the grand prize! 

Register now and get an exclusive discount! 

We know that learning is vital – now more than ever. As an SAP TechEd Virtual registrant, you can take advantage of an exciting package of curated learning, engagement, and solution experience offerings, including a 25% discount on SAP Learning Hub. 

Register today for SAP TechEd Virtual, secure your discount and visit the SAP TechEd Virtual – Learning zone, where you can acquire in-demand skills and prepare for a globally recognized certification with self-paced learning to boost your career. 

See you all at SAP TechEd Virtual!

entry.title: Elevate Your Career: Become an SAP-Certified Solution Architect for SAP BTP
entry.content:
In the rapidly evolving technology landscape, businesses are constantly searching for ways to stay ahead of the competition and drive innovation. For IT professionals, this shift presents a golden opportunity: becoming an SAP BTP Solution Architect. By mastering SAP Business Technology Platform, a unified, integrated offering that transforms the way organizations approach digital transformation, you position yourself as a pivotal force in helping organizations unlock new efficiencies, innovate faster, and achieve their business goals. 

Your pathway to success 

As companies rapidly shift to cloud-based solutions, there is an increasing demand for skilled professionals who can bridge the gap between business needs and technology capabilities. As SAP BTP Solution Architect, you play a crucial role in this transformation journey, guiding businesses through integration, extension, analytics, and data management. And with a brand-new role-based certification, we are helping you to achieve this goal.

Check out our brand-new role-based certification SAP Certified Professional - Solution Architect - SAP BTP. This certification validates that you possess the ability to perform an SAP Solution Architect role with SAP Business Technology Platform. By getting certified, you demonstrate that you can develop and implement solutions that meet business requirements and translate requirements of an organization into technical solution designs.

Start with this free learning journey 

Getting started is easy. Just look at the new free, self-paced learning journey "Becoming an SAP BTP Solution Architect" on the SAP Learning site. After completing this learning journey, you will have a comprehensive understanding of the tools and methods required to effectively use SAP BTP to envision and design an enterprise architecture. You will be equipped with the skills to align IT strategies with business objectives using SAP BTP tools and technologies.  

Open new professional opportunities for you with new learning and a sought-after certification. Build the skills you need on the SAP Learning site and maximize your learning experience by joining the SAP Learning Groups, to ask questions from SAP experts and connect with fellow learners. When you’re ready, take your certification exam and start shaping your industry's future!

entry.title: Build the Skills to Bring out Your Best: SAP TechEd Virtual – Learning Zone Is Now Live!
entry.content:
Calling all tech enthusiasts! With SAP TechEd Virtual being just around the corner, it’s time to get ahead of the game and build the skills to bring out your best. Join a community of experts, acquire in-demand skills, and prepare for a globally recognized certification with self-paced learning in the SAP TechEd Virtual – Learning zone – even before the main event starts on October 8!

Learning tools and opportunities for you:

  • Get started with 40 interactive value journeys and showcases to discover the value of SAP solutions.
  • Access 45 free, self-paced learning journeys curated to each of the content tracks of SAP TechEd Virtual to upskill in the latest SAP solutions. 
  • Subscribe to premium, guided learning resources with SAP Learning Hub to maximize your skills and earn a globally recognized certification, with an exclusive 25% discount.
  • Access demos and 25 basic trials to explore the power of SAP solutions and 50 use cases to get hands-on experience.
  • Connect with experts, expand your network, and collaborate with peers in dedicated learning groups and bridge the time until October 8 with SAP Community events.

Explore learning offerings for each SAP TechEd track:

  • Digital Transformation with Cloud ERP 
  • SAP Business Technology Platform
  • Artificial Intelligence
  • Application Development and Automation
  • Data and Analytics
  • Integration

Register for SAP TechEd Virtual to benefit from a 25% discount on SAP Learning Hub:

 As a registered SAP TechEd Virtual attendee, you can benefit from an exclusive 25% discount on your SAP Learning Hub subscription! Once you have subscribed to our guided premium learning offering, you can get access to learning content and certification exam attempts, expert guidance in digital live sessions and on-demand hands-on practice systems too.

We know that learning is vital – now more than ever. Jump to the SAP TechEd Virtual – Learning zone today to get a head start into SAP TechEd in 2024.

See you all at SAP TechEd Virtual – Learning zone, and beyond!

entry.title: Hear from SAP Champion Amin Omidy How Lifelong Learning Can Unlock Your Career Potential
entry.content:
@Amin_Omidyhas been reading SAP white papers and SAP Community posts for as long as he can remember, covering various areas of interest for the Solution and Enterprise Architect who works as a consultant for clients. “I am always fascinated by the rich information and great answers to different questions related to SAP implementation”, he explains. “I subscribed to different topics to receive notifications via email and actively search for mature SAP answers or related SAP notes to help me with my issues.” 

With more than ten years of experience in SAP solutions for both private and public sectors, Amin certainly knows the pain points of his customers – and how to solve them. “My expertise lies in helping businesses transition to the cloud and establishing modern ERP implementations. This can involve starting an ERP project from scratch or enhancing existing customer solutions, such as migrating on-premise solutions to the cloud or integrating new Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) applications into their IT landscape”, he says. Where else but in the SAP Community could he find answers to such complex issues?  

SAP Learning site helps solve complex problems

Amin: "I am always fascinated by the rich information and great answers the SAP Community provides related to SAP implementation."Well, there’s also the SAP Learning site, Amin admits. “Your website offers a wealth of up-to-date and practical insights, making it an invaluable resource for staying current with the latest SAP developments and best practices.” What he values the most, according to the Canadian resident, is the structured and detailed content helping him solve complex problems and enhancing his understanding of SAP solutions. “My passion to enable businesses to streamline their operations drives me to stay updated with the latest technological advancements”, he concludes.  

However, it’s not only his passion towards his customers that drives Amin to stay at the forefront of innovation. He is convinced: “Living in an era dominated by IT technologies like VMs, microservices, cloud computing, and AI, continuous learning has become essential.” According to Amin, enablement and engagement provided from SAP has significantly enhanced his ability to serve customers better. “Each new piece of knowledge or technique I acquire not only boosts my professional skills but also brings a sense of accomplishment when applied in real-world scenarios.” Watching the positive results and customer satisfaction would confirm the value of lifelong learning and its impact on both his career and personal growth.  

Making impact as SAP Champion

Maybe that’s one of the reasons why Amin also enjoys mentoring young professionals and participating in webinars to share knowledge and learn from others, even outside of work. And why he became an SAP Champion. He simply loves engaging with others, be it his customers or peers in the SAP Community. “Whether through writing blogs, addressing customer issues and potential solutions or serving as a voice for customers within the SAP ecosystem, it allows me to actively contribute and make a meaningful impact”, he recently stated in an interview.  

In return, Amin gets a lot back: “Learning something new through the SAP Community has been incredibly rewarding. The reliability and depth of the information provided are unmatched, ensuring that I can trust the knowledge I gain and apply it directly to my daily work. The opportunity to interact with bloggers or writers to verify any part of the answer or get more related details is particularly valuable. This engagement helps me stay updated with the latest best practices and solutions, enhancing my skills and efficiency in handling SAP-related tasks. The experience is both exciting and encouraging, continually expanding my understanding and capabilities within the SAP ecosystem.” 

Start your own success story today

If you want to stay informed, connected, and up to date with the latest innovations, explore the SAP Community and head over to SAP Learning site to quickly learn a new skill, upskill, and get certified. For access to our guided, premium learning resources, consider a subscription to SAP Learning Hub.

entry.title: New Study Quantifies the Benefits of Continuous Learning with SAP Learning Hub and SAP Enable Now
entry.content:
For businesses, ensuring business success in times of rapid digital transformation isn’t easy. But if you work in the technology sector, staying up to date with the latest innovations is equally important for you and your career growth. While there are many ways to upskill or reskill, many professionals turn to SAP learning solutions. And that’s great – here’s why: 

A recent study now* shows that upskilling with SAP Learning Hub and SAP Enable Now helps customers gain the right skills critical to drive digital transformation – while also ensuring project success and maximizing the return on investment your organizations made with SAP solutions. Keep reading to learn more about why future-focused SAP learning solutions could be the right path for you and your employer and how SAP customers have benefited from their investment. 

Who conducted the study and how was it done? 

The study on the power of continuous upskilling with SAP Learning Hub and SAP Enable Now was conducted by Forrester Consulting, a leading global market research company, on behalf of SAP in 2024. As organizations undergo digital transformation, we sought to understand how our offerings are helping customers and partners to ensure business success and examine the potential return on their investment (ROI) by using SAP Learning Hub and SAP Enable Now.  

For their research, Forrester used their Total Economic Impact (TEI) methodology, a trusted industry standard that models all aspects of a technology or solution and the associated impacts on the business. Focusing on quantifying benefits, Forrester analyzed costs, flexibility and more. Through interviews with 12 experienced SAP customers, they provided a comprehensive study backed by solid data on the value of SAP’s offerings. 

What problems did learners and organizations have? 

Interviewees said that prior to using SAP Enable Now or adopting SAP Learning Hub, their organizations relied on legacy approaches to provide training and enablement content for their employees. Advanced SAP users such as project leads, and IT team members even lacked quality SAP-specific content and internal learning tools. In-person, instructor-led classes and onsite consultants were valuable but not realistic for all employees given the time and cost commitments. These limitations led to knowledge gaps and inefficiencies for typical end users and advanced users alike. 

What changed for learners after SAP Learning Hub or SAP Enable Now were implemented? 

As one training lead from the transportation sector put it: “SAP Learning Hub has all the resources that you need in one system. Our employees can upskill and also get the promotion they want.” In solid data points, Forrester presented the following benefits of using SAP Learning Hub and SAP Enable Now to upskill: 

  • Improved upskilling due to greater learning access, resulting in productivity gains of up to 25%: As a source of extensive, high-quality, and digitally accessible guided SAP learning resources for those in more expert roles, SAP Learning Hub equipped users with the knowledge they need to stay up to speed and learn new skills. 
  • Faster time to competence by 50% for end users: SAP Enable Now provided end users at the composite organization representative of interviewed customers with updated, easy-to-follow content and assistance directly in the flow of work.  

Interviewees reported that after the investment in SAP Learning Hub and SAP Enable Now, their workforce became more knowledgeable, confident, and productive. Key results from the investment included improved upskilling for advanced SAP users, cost savings, faster time to competence for end users, more efficient content creation, and greater project success. 

How can you benefit from SAP Learning Hub and SAP Enable Now? 

That’s easy! If you’re looking for future-focused, high-quality, and digitally accessible upskilling, you can rest assured that SAP Learning Hub is the right thing to turn to. With a subscription to the enhanced SAP Learning Hub, you can get access to all resources you need to prepare for certification, take the exams with four included attempts and stay certified – at better value than ever before.  

Where can I learn more about getting and staying certified with SAP Learning Hub? 

To learn more about the ways to get and stay certified, visit this FAQ. Take a deep dive into the Forrester TEI study, click here. To learn more about SAP Learning Hub, visit this page. 

*July 2024 Forrester Consulting case study commissioned by SAP titled: The Total Economic Impact ™ of SAP Learning Hub and SAP Enable now. Read the full study. Results are for a composite organization.

entry.title: Hear from SAP Champion Kunal Bansal 🏅: How SAP TechEd Virtual 2024 Can Help Boost Your Career
entry.content:
When @KunalBansal booked his hotel in Bangalore near the 2023 SAP TechEd venue, he knew he wouldn’t be spending much time there. Instead, his real focus was on the learning, networking, and upskilling at SAP’s premier technology event - TechEd. “Learn, share, grow - that’s the motto I believe in,” Kunal says, reflecting on his experience. Attending SAP TechEd in-person for the 2nd time, he discovered just how powerful in-person networking and knowledge-sharing could be. “It’s the best place to connect, ask questions, and exchange ideas,” Kunal emphasizes. 

His enthusiasm shines through as he recalls the inspiring keynote delivered by SAP Board Members, which focused on the transformative potential of GenAI. He also cherishes the time spent at the SAP Community booth, engaging with fellow SAP Mentors and Champions. “I’m definitely attending again this year,” he says with a smile. 

With SAP TechEd Virtual 2024 on October 8-9 just weeks away, Kunal is busy curating his agenda. He’s carefully selecting sessions that will provide the most value, both professionally and personally. 

Kunal's Top Picks for SAP TechEd Virtual in 2024:

  • XP101 | SAP BTP strategy and highlights  
    Get firsthand insights into SAP’s BTP strategy and gain a deeper understanding of how SAP BTP unifies development, automation, data, and AI into one powerful platform, driving digital transformation. 
  • IN100 | Innovate now with a modern, AI-assisted integration suite 
    Discover how to accelerate innovation by integrating business systems using SAP Integration Suite, enabling real-time, scalable enterprise-wide integration.  
  • IN103 | Modernize and elevate to the cloud with SAP Integration Suite 
    Learn how to transition smoothly from legacy systems to the SAP cloud, leveraging SAP Integration Suite’s robust tools, best practices and services for a seamless migration. 

Make sure you also check out Kunal's entire expert session playlist for SAP TechEd Virtual!

SAP Learning site is his go-to resource

“Learn, share, grow - that’s the motto I believe in,” Kunal says.During the week, Kunal is fully immersed in his role as an Advisory Architect at IBM, leveraging over 13 years of experience in SAP CX Suites. His deep expertise in SAP Intelligent Enterprise and SAP Solutions underscores his commitment to staying current in the field.

“I work closely with stakeholders to deliver scalable, compliant, and high-performing solutions using SAP technologies,” Kunal explains. His role demands a unique blend of technical expertise, strategic thinking, and strong communication skills to drive successful project outcomes. “And to stay current, I dedicate at least an hour daily to learning.” 

Kunal often turns to the SAP Learning site to stay updated with the rapidly evolving technologies. “The free SAP Learning portal has been my go-to resource for learning journeys, leading to SAP Certification, while Learning Hub and Partner Edge portals have deepened my concepts to deliver greater business value for clients". 

Strengthened credibility, solidified reputation

While dedicating an hour a day to learning can seem daunting at first, Kunal says it’s worth it, as it has become a crucial part of his journey, pushing him to achieve greater heights and inspiring others. “Earning SAP Certifications through continuous learning has been a game-changer for my career growth, unlocking opportunities for more complex, high-impact projects”. On a more personal level, Kunal notes, “SAP Certifications have strengthened my credibility and solidified my reputation as a thought leader in the SAP Community.” 

As Kunal finalizes his SAP TechEd Virtual 2024 agenda, he’s also exploring the recently launched SAP TechEd Virtual Learning Zone to discover further learning tools and opportunities mapped to the six SAP TechEd Virtual content tracks. “I’m excited to dive into Developing with SAP Integration Suite to keep myself engaged until SAP TechEd Virtual kicks off,” Kunal says, eagerly anticipating the innovations in SAP Business AI and SAP BTP. 

Now it's your time to shine!

What sessions are you most excited about, and what’s your favorite resource from the SAP TechEd Virtual - Learning Zone? Share your thoughts in the comments and make sure to visit the SAP TechEd group on SAP Community to connect with peers!

entry.title: Heatwave of Innovation: A Glimpse Into the 2024 Premium Hub CoE STEM High School Summer Orientation
entry.content:
This summer, a group of bright high school students embarked on an exciting journey at SAP's week-long STEM orientation program. This program focused on harnessing the power of cloud-based AI apps using the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), providing students with practical, hands-on learning experiences using low-code/no-code tools.

 

The week kicked off with students registering for their SAP accounts and setting up trial accounts on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). A key component of the learning experience was SAP Build Apps, a powerful tool within SAP BTP that enabled students to design and develop applications using a low-code/no-code approach. This tool simplified the app creation process, allowing students to focus on functionality and creativity rather than complex coding.

The primary task was to create a barcode scanner application using SAP Build Apps. This app scanned barcodes and retrieved detailed information about food items from the Open Food Facts API, a free and open database of food product information. This integration taught students how to connect their applications to external data sources, demonstrating the potential of combining SAP BTP with other digital resources.

 

 

The real magic happened when students were introduced to Google Gemini, allowing them to enhance their applications with Generative AI capabilities. By integrating AI into their apps, students were able to analyze the health aspects of food ingredients, transforming simple barcode scanners into intelligent, interactive applications. This exercise encouraged students to think critically about how AI can enhance user experiences and provide valuable insights.

As the week progressed, students eagerly worked on their projects, each bringing their own flair to the task. They showcased the culmination of their hard work in a demo session held on Friday, presenting their enhanced applications. It was inspiring to see how each participant leveraged the tools and guidance provided to create something truly remarkable.

The orientation week was not just about building apps; it was about fostering a spirit of innovation and collaboration. The students left with more than just technical skills; they gained confidence and a deeper understanding of how technology can be used to solve real-world problems.

The feedback from participants has been overwhelmingly positive, with many expressing how the program has sparked a new interest in technology and AI. Here's what one of our students had to say about their unforgettable experience:

Participating in this program was an invaluable experience. I had the chance to meet many new people and delve deeply into SAP, low-coding program development, and generative AI. This program pushed me out of my comfort zone and provided a valuable glimpse into the future of IT. I appreciated the opportunity to grow, learn, and envision a career in this dynamic field. - Riddhi Bakde

This orientation was a testament to the power of education and technology in shaping the minds of tomorrow's innovators. As we continue to provide these opportunities, we are excited to see how these young minds will contribute to the ever-evolving tech landscape.

 

Thank you to Balaji Rao Gaddam for sponsoring and hosting this year’s event.  

Thank you to the SAP colleagues who coordinated this year’s Summer Orientation and made it a success!

Ashok Kumar M & Megan Rech (Leads) Jared Durkin (Project Co-Coordinator), Luke Korkuis (HR) and volunteers and speakers. A huge thank you to our onsite team coordinators Oswayne Smith, Nkem Ohanenye, Cecily Devaney, Nivi Rao and Jacy Yu.

And a special thanks to the SAP speakers and colleagues who shared their expertise!
John Astill (D-Shop Demo), Evan Taylor (STAR Internship Program), Justin Lynch (SAP Overview & Career Development), George Harwood (NSQ Campus Tour), Safwanur Rahman & Vivek RR (Artificial Intelligence Experts), Rakesh Gandhi and Matt Ruff (Industry 4.0 Center Tour), Manuel Gruber (MCC Presentation)

 

If you have any questions or feedback about the Summer Orientation, please contact Megan Rech.

entry.title: New SAP SuccessFactors Certifications for Certified Individuals in SAP HCM (C_THR12) Available
entry.content:
The shift to cloud computing has undoubtedly changed the way organizations carry out their HR operations. For SAP users, the cloud-based future of Human Capital Management lies in the SAP SuccessFactors solution.

That is also why the maintenance period for SAP Business Suite 7 (ECC) and the previous SAP ERP HCM system as an on-premise solution ends on December 31, 2027. If you are already operating the SAP HCM solution in the Compatibility Pack (CP) with SAP S/4HANA, the usage rights will end on December 31, 2025. As a result, the SAP Certification SAP Certified Associate - SAP HCM for S/4HANA (C_THR12) will be discontinued on March 31, 2025. You can find the planned retirement date on the certification website. Certified users will stay certified for 12 months after the certification retirement. The final due date will be visible on My Learning Certifications (sap.com). Please also note that the academies THR10 and THR12 a– the trainings to prepare for the certification, will be discontinued by the end of Q1, 2025. 

But don’t worry about the retirement! To ensure you stay on top of your HR game, SAP stepped up and continues to launch future-focused earning and enablement resources for you, making sure you excel in a cloud-first HR world. On the SAP Learning site, you can find tailored, bite-sized learning and certification opportunities for the SAP SuccessFactors solution, including topics like recruiting, workforce analytics and planning, talent management, payroll, and more. A few highlights include: 

Learning journeys for beginners: 

  • SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Core 
  • Exploring the SAP SuccessFactors Platform 

 SAP Certifications:

  • SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP SuccessFactors Compensation 

To learn more about certification retirements, check out this FAQ. To learn more about the ways to get and stay certified, visit this FAQ. Browse all available SAP Certifications here.

entry.title: Accelerating Digital Transformation: An Insider's Journey to SAP Cloud ALM Mastery
entry.content:
“It just makes work easier and helps you to better understand the designed processes”, Martina Ohlerth says. The IT engineer and product owner works at REWE digital in Germany and oversees the further development of the cloud-based tool for application lifecycle management (ALM), which is available as part of SAP support offerings, covering all stages of the application lifecycle, from discovery of cloud solution capabilities to implementation, operations, and continuous innovation as well as business value realization and enhancement.

“We wanted to preserve our investment with SAP Cloud ALM” 

 With more than 18 years of experience in the field, the 42-year-old has experienced digital transformation first-hand. “To date, we have already achieved a lot with SAP Solution Manager to be more transparent in application lifecycle management. We want to preserve this investment with SAP Cloud ALM.”, Martina explains. “This is the main reason why we are intensively investigating SAP's new tool." 

The mother of three and her small team of four colleagues that is sometimes supported by external consultants embarked on the SAP Cloud ALM journey one and a half years ago. To learn more about the strategy behind the solution, and to find out if it was the right fit for the company, Martina turned to the German SAP-user group DSAG and one of their conferences. “This helped us a lot to find out where we could be going with this”, she explains. 

First learning journey developed with SAP partner  

Liking what they saw, the team began with training for the standard processes. “We turned to live sessions at SAP, looked at the SAP Community and more to get the latest information”, Martina says. When she heard about the beginner-level learning journey “Implementing with SAP Cloud ALM”, available on the SAP Learning site, she immediately saw the potential for everyone on her team to benefit from the free learning resource.  

Created together with SAP partner Vostura, the free, self-paced course is the first that has been developed in collaboration, allowing learners to gain all the necessary knowledge to use SAP Cloud ALM in agile implementation projects – in just under 10 hours. The activities were the starting point for the team's participation in the classroom training “SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation“ to gain direct experience through extensive system exercises with SAP Cloud ALM. 

“In my line of business, it is vital to stay up-to-date with the latest innovations”, Martina highlights, recommending continuous learning for everyone, not just her team. “It just makes work easier and helps you to better understand the designed processes”, she concludes.   

What’s in it for you? 

Are you looking to boost your career and kick-start your own success story? Benefit from tailored and self-paced learning opportunities that meet you when and where you learn best on the SAP Learning site. Get certified to demonstrate your expertise and boost your career.
